texto: It’s YOUR journey. Ask yourself who you are, where you want to go, and how you’d like to get there. Graciously accept advice from everyone, but don’t look left and right and wonder why you’re not taking the path of your peers. Enjoy the path you’re on and make the most of it. Carpe College!
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Michael Metzler: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de temas ligados a motivacao. O trecho “It’s YOUR journey. Ask yourself who you are, where you want to go, and how you’d like to get ther…” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que motivacao, college, college-life pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de viagem e inspiracao.

frase_original: It’s YOUR journey. Ask yourself who you are, where you want to go, and how you’d like to get there. Graciously accept advice from everyone, but don’t look left and right and wonder why you’re not taking the path of your peers. Enjoy the path you’re on and make the most of it. Carpe College!

texto: For this very short time in your life, and possibly never again, you will be given a very unique gift: a supremely rich environment with ample time, space, safety and people to explore whatever interests you intensely, to push yourself, to make mistakes and recover from them, and to live as deeply as you'd like in the hope of finding yourself or, at least, the beginnings of yourself.Such an opportunity may never present itself again. So, embrace it and.... Carpe College!
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Michael Metzler: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de limites e escolhas conscientes. O trecho “For this very short time in your life, and possibly never again, you will be given a very unique …” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que vida, college, college-life pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de inspiracao e college-life.
